sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁאַל (Syr. ܫܶܐܠ, Arab. سَأَلَ).

(1) to ask, to demand, followed by an acc. of the thing, 1Sa 12:13 and מִן Psa 2:8 מֵאֵת 1Sa 8:10, of the person from whom any thing is asked, also followed by two acc. (αἰτεῖν τινά τι, to ask any one any thing), Psa 137:3 Deu 14:26 Isa 45:11. Job 31:31, לִשְׁאֹול בְּאָלָה נַפְשֹׁו “by asking with a curse his (an enemy’s) life,” i.e. for his death, comp. Jon 4:8, וַיִּשְׁאַל אֶת־נַפְשֹׁו לָמוּת “and he desired death for himself;” 1Ki 19:4.

(2) to ask, to request any thing from any one, Isa 7:11 (see עָמַק Hiphil), שָׁאַל שְׁאֵלָה see שְׁאֵלָה . Followed by an acc. of thing, Jud. 5:25 1Ki 3:10 מִן of the person from whom it is asked, Psa 21:5 also מֵאֵת 1Ki 2:20 מֵעִים Deu 18:16 שָׁאַל דָּבָר לְ to ask any thing for any one, 1Ki 2:22.

Specially-(a) to ask a loan, to borrow from any one, Exo 3:22 11:2 12:35 . Part. שָׁאוּל lent, 1Sa 1:28 2Ki 6:5. Followed by לְ prob. to lend, i.q. Hiphil, 1Sa 2:20.-(b) to ask alms, to beg, i.q. Piel No. 1, Pro 20:4. (Arab. Conj. V. id., ساَيِءلُ a beggar.)

(3) to inquire of, to interrogate, Jud. 4:20 seq.; followed by an acc. of pers., Gen 24:47 Job 40:7 Jos 9:14, “but they inquired not at the mouth of Jehovah” (which they ought to have done); compare Isa 30:2 Gen 24:57 followed by לְ of pers., Job 8:8. The person or thing asked about, has לְ before it, Jud. 13:18 Gen 32:30 עַל Neh 1:2 acc., Hag 2:11 Isa 45:11. Specially it is-(a) to consult; followed by בְּ (fich befragen ben), as שָׁאַל בַּיהֹוָה to consult the oracle of Jehovah, Jud 1:1 18:5 20:18 שָׁאַל בַּתְּרָפִים to consult Teraphim, Eze 21:26 Followed by לְ for any one, for his benefit, 1Sa 22:10, 13 1Sa 22:13, 15 1Sa 22:15 Num 27:21.-(b) שָׁאַל לִפְ׳ לְשָׁלֹום to ask of any one’s health; hence to salute, Gen 43:27 1Sa 10:4 17:22 30:21 Exo 18:7.

Niphal, to ask for oneself, to ask leave (like the Gr. αἰτοῦμαί σε τοῦτο; Germ. fich etwas ausbitten; followed by מִן of pers. and a gerund of the thing, to do which one asks for leave, 1Sa 20:6, 28 1Sa 20:28(where the verb is omitted); followed by a finite verb, Neh 13:6. Comp. Gram. § 50, 2. Others take it, to obtain by prayers, leave or liberty from a master; Germ. fich fren=losbitten.

Piel שִׁאֵל-

(1) to beg, to be a beggar, i.q. Kal No. 2, b; Psa 109:10.

(2) to question, 2Sa 20:18.

Hiphil, to lend, Exo 12:36 1Sa 1:28 compare Kal No. 2, a.

Derivatives, שְׁאֵלָה, מִשְׁאָלָה; and the pr.n. שְׁאָל, שָׁאוּל, שֵׁלָה שְׁאַלְתִּיאֵל, אֶשְׁתָּאֹול.

II.
